channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Rising gas prices are good news for EV sales, for now
item.description: It's become an American truism: Once you buy an SUV, there's no going back. And our love affair with pickups has been documented in a million country songs and TV commercials. But a brutal spike in gasoline prices - nipping $4 nationwide for a gallon of unleaded, up from $3 when the bombs first began […]

item.title: Amazon veut s’offrir le réseau satellite utilisé par Apple pour rivaliser avec Starlink
item.description: La guerre de l'internet spatial prend une tournure inattendue. Amazon lorgne très sérieusement sur Globalstar, l'entreprise historique qui permet à vos iPhone de capter des signaux de détresse en pleine pampa. Seul hic pour conclure ce rachat monumental : Apple possède une grosse part du gâteau et ne compte pas se laisser faire.

item.title: Could Dave Filoni’s ‘Star Wars’ Cameo Have an Important Role in ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’?
item.description: Plus, Wagner Moura is stepping in for Oscar Isaac in Panos Cosmatos' new vampire movie.

item.title: Soundcore Nebula X1 Pro review: The king of party projectors
item.description: Every now and then, I test a gadget so wild that I can’t believe a company actually made it. Soundcore’s $5,000 Nebula X1 Pro projector is the embodiment of that: an ultra bright projector and a 400-watt Dolby Atmos 7.1 speaker system combined in a massive enclosure. With a fast and flexible setup, it lets you screen movies or watch sports nearly anywhere. It’s not just a projector crammed into a big speaker system, though. Everything is elegantly integrated and setup is nearly automatic, thanks to the clever design and motorization. The weight and price are the biggest strikes against it, but...

item.title: Transfer Point is a modern adventure game made with 40-year-old software
item.description: One of the year's most intriguing games was developed using software first released 40 years ago. Transfer Point looks and plays like a classic Mac point-and-click adventure game, and there's a very good reason for that: It was developed using World Builder, a game creation tool first released in 1986 that has since become freeware. […]

item.title: Exclusive: I put the foldable Honor Magic V6 in a washing machine to test its durability — here's what happened next
item.description: The Honor Magic V6 is the first folding phone with IP69 water resistance, so I tested that claim by putting it in the washing machine.

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 price hikes are 'inevitable', says former Nintendo sales lead — and that's not an exaggeration
item.description: PC and game console price hikes are widespread, and it looks like the Nintendo Switch 2 may be the next to see a significant price adjustment.

item.title: 'The upgraded PSSR significantly enhances Assassin’s Creed Shadows' — Sony confirms a release date for its next-gen upscaling tech on PS5 Pro
item.description: Sony has announced that its upgraded PSSR AI upscaling technology will finally be added to Assassin's Creed Shadows this month.

item.title: Exclusive: Beehiiv expands into podcasting, taking aim at Patreon
item.description: One way Beehiiv is convincing creators to switch from rivals like Substack and Patreon is by not taking a cut of revenue.

item.title: Le Galaxy S26 a un gros problème d’autonomie, on vous explique
item.description: Le Galaxy S26 présente une autonomie inférieure à celle du S25, avec seulement 16h37 d'usage continu contre 18h26. Malgré une batterie plus grande de 4300 mAh, la puce Exynos 2600 semble consommer 40% d'énergie en plus que son concurrent Snapdragon.

item.title: Le tout nouveau MacBook Air 13 propulsé par la puce M5 profite déjà de 100 € de réduction
item.description: Le tout nouveau MacBook Air 13, véritable star de la gamme, arrive en 2026 avec une puce M5. Cet ultraportable léger et performant s’affiche déjà en promotion à 1 099 euros au lieu de 1 199 euros, de quoi craquer sans attendre.

item.title: Artemis II : voici le bracelet connecté ultra-perfectionné que portent les astronautes pour retourner vers la Lune
item.description: Pour accompagner le retour historique de l'homme vers la Lune, la Nasa a équipé les astronautes d'Artemis II de bracelets connectés issus du projet ARCHeR. Ces dispositifs permettent de surveiller en temps réel la santé et le stress de l'équipage afin d'optimiser les futurs systèmes de survie de la capsule Orion.

item.title: On a testé le Ugreen Maxidok 17-en-1 : 17 ports et un slot SSD pour brancher tout votre PC avec un seul câble
item.description: 500 euros pour un dock ? Ça paraît fou, mais le Ugreen Maxidok est une bête de course. On l'a testé.

item.title: 'This is not an April Fool's joke': Crypto platform Drift suspends services after millions stolen
item.description: So far, no one has claimed responsibility for the attack in which $280 million were pulled from Drift.

item.title: De l’ADN pour chiffrer ses données : au Japon, Macron a testé cette technologie réputée inviolable, comment ça marche ?
item.description: Une équipe de chercheurs franco-japonaise a développé une nouvelle technique utilisant de l’ADN synthétique pour sécuriser les communications avec un chiffrement quasi inviolable.

item.title: "Protecting children online is a parental responsibility, not a regulatory one" — the VPN industry reacts to Government VPN spending amid discussions on restricting child VPN use
item.description: Following our exclusive research, how does the VPN industry see the UK Government's actions?

item.title: Windows en danger : les pirates peuvent désactiver l’antivirus sans déclencher d’alerte
item.description: Pour faire sauter l'antivirus de votre ordinateur Windows, les cybercriminels se servent de plus en plus d'outils d'administration légitimes. Ces outils permettent aux hackers d'agir sans se faire repérer.

item.title: The ‘Deadpool & Wolverine’ Moment Designed for You to Gawk at Hugh Jackman’s Chiseled Body Is Now an Action Figure
item.description: Need more shirtless Hugh Jackman in your life in increasingly specific contexts? Hasbro has you covered.

item.title: Cash App launches ‘pay later’ feature for P2P pay transfers
item.description: Block says that the new feature has strong built-in protections to keep users from so-called debt spirals.

item.title: 'I'm not missing the four hours of daily prosthetics': For All Mankind season 5 star on 'challenges' in new Apple TV show Imperfect Women
item.description: With For All Mankind season 5 and Imperfect Women both on Apple TV, Joel Kinnaman is having the last laugh — but there have still been 'challenges' along the way.

item.title: The hottest EVs from the 2026 New York Auto Show (plus one brawny concept)
item.description: With gas prices rising across the country, consumers are turning to electric vehicles as a way to save money on their commute. And while there weren’t a ton of all-new EVs on display at the 2026 New York International Auto Show, we did see some notable debuts from automakers including Subaru, Kia, Hyundai and more. So here’s a look at some of the most interesting upcoming EV models on display today, including a handful of previously announced vehicles that we haven’t had a chance to see in person before. Subaru GetawaySadly, Toyota didn't bring the Highlander EV to the NY Auto Show, so I could...

item.title: National Burrito Day freebees and deals: Where to get your Mexican food fix today, from Chipotle to Del Tacos
item.description: From BOGO deals and free delivery to discounted meals, restaurant chains are offering ways to celebrate the gloriousness of the burrito. Burritos should be celebrated, because they are ingenious inventions that wrap deliciousness in a handy tortilla for extra convenience. National Burrito Day (today, Thursday, April 2, 2026) was created to do just that. ...

item.title: Apple's controversial Fitness VP Jay Blahnik is retiring
item.description: Jay Blahnik, who served as Apple’s Fitness chief for almost 13 years, has announced that he’s retiring this July. According to The New York Times, Blahnik told employees in an email that he was leaving “to spend time with his family and make an exciting move to New York City.” Blahnik is retiring less than a year after The Times reported on allegations that he was "verbally abusive, manipulative and inappropriate” towards his employees, creating a toxic workplace environment. Approximately 10 out of the 100 employees under his leadership had reportedly sought extended leaves of absence for men...

item.title: Visa says AI could start making purchases for you. Not everyone wants that, but here’s how close we are
item.description: New research from the company suggests most people are comfortable letting AI help them shop. Letting AI buy things is another matter. What if you didn’t actually decide to buy that last thing in your cart?A report from Visa released on Thursday suggests that, in some cases, you might not have.

item.title: Revirement chez Volkswagen : les boutons physiques font leur grand retour, c’est « non négociable »
item.description: Volkswagen fait définitivement machine arrière sur les commandes tactiles. Jugées trop peu pratiques par les utilisateurs, elles laisseront systématiquement place aux boutons physiques dans les futurs modèles.

item.title: Best Lego Gifts for Brick Builders (2026): Smart Bricks, Video Games, and More
item.description: Your kids are only kids for so long, but love and Lego sets last forever. Here are our top picks for the Lego fan in your life.

item.title: A New Implant Aims to Rewire Stroke Patients’ Brains
item.description: Epia Neuro’s brain-computer interface will include a motorized glove to help stroke patients recover movement in their hand.
